name: publish
context: Publish Dojo nodes - version correctly, authenticate, and release to a registry
info: Publishing is the write-side release flow for Dojo nodes. It should come after schema validation, knowledge validation, and a local smoke test, and it should treat version strings as immutable release artifacts. The package-style publish scripts under this node are tested so the verified status is about the actual executable entry files, not just the manifest prose. The portable package is now complete enough for another agent runtime to dry-run, rehearse, and publish without reverse-engineering the workflow from `node.json` alone.
triggers: how do i publish to dojo
body:
# Publish Dojo Nodes

Publishing should happen after validation and a local smoke test. Treat version strings as immutable release artifacts, not mutable drafts.

This skill uses a package-style `entry` script so the release behavior can be tested and reused. Use [[dojo/publish/local]] to rehearse the release against a local registry and [[dojo/publish/registry]] when you are ready to POST the manifest.

sections.title: Versioning
sections.body: If the content changes, bump the version. Never reuse a published version string for different content.

sections.title: Auth
sections.body: Registry publish is an authenticated operation. Keep the registry token separate from the manifest itself and inject it at publish time.

sections.title: Release flow
sections.body: Run [[dojo/validate]], rehearse with [[dojo/publish/local]], then publish with [[dojo/publish/registry]]. Only after the node resolves, learns, and bundles correctly should you consider the release complete.

sections.title: Post-publish checks
sections.body: After release, verify the node through `/v1/search`, `/v1/skills/*`, and `/v1/learn/*`. Publishing without a read-side check leaves you blind to integration regressions.

links.context: Validation workflow that should happen first

related.note: Publishing should only happen after validation passes
The API node documents the contract behind publish requests

depends.reason: Publishing should depend on successful validation

scripts.name: Publish Node
scripts.description: Publish a manifest to a Dojo registry endpoint

DOJO_DRY_RUN.description: Whether to emit a dry-run preview instead of a live publish

author: dojo-community
